page: Microsoft Azure E48as_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine belonging to the Easv7 server family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores using AMD EPYC 9V45 processors running at 4.5 GHz. The system features 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute and memory capabilities. Single-core performance is in the top 10% for stress-ng and compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. Multi-core performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the top 25%. Memory subsystem benchmarks show a strong top 25% performance for cached small-block reads and large-block writes, though uncached large-block read bandwidth drops to the middle 50%. Database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the top 25%, reflecting efficient transaction handling.

From a resource density perspective, the E48as_v7 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. The lack of local storage means all disk I/O relies on network-attached storage, which introduces latency tradeoffs. Additionally, while small-model LLM inference prompt processing ranks in the top 10%, scaling to medium 7B models drops prompt processing performance to the middle 50%, indicating limits for larger AI workloads without dedicated accelerators.

This server configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, including relational and non-relational databases, in-memory caching layers such as Redis, enterprise application hosting, and data processing pipelines. It also supports moderate web serving and lightweight machine learning inference tasks that fit within the CPU and memory profile.
description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Easv7 family, powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on remote Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top 10% single-core CPU and compression performance, alongside top 25% multi-core and database execution speeds. However, uncached large-block memory read bandwidth remains in the middle 50%. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy workloads by avoiding the premium of local NVMe or GPU hardware. It is suited for database operations, in-memory caching, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48as_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, designed for databases and in-memory caching.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual machine offering high memory density and strong single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 9V45 processors.
Top 10% single-core CPU and data compression benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU, requiring remote Premium Storage.
Strong top 25% database and Redis SET operation throughput.
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications.
